title: Nuvama Sector Report: India's Financial Sector at Crossroads (Jun 2026)
duplicate_stories: RBI's FCNR (B) special deposit window (8 Jun-30 Sep 2026, RBI-borne hedging cost, CRR/SLR exemption, USD50-100bn expected inflow) — substantially overlaps the 10 Jun 2026 note 'FCNR-B 2026: Bank Rate Signals, IRR Dynamics & Forex Reserve Mobilisation'
executive_summary: Nuvama's 210-page FY27 outlook (initiating/assuming coverage on 30 banks, NBFCs and fintechs) frames India's financial sector 'at crossroads': West-Asia conflict, El Nino and slower GDP (6.3% FY27E vs 7.8% FY26) are macro headwinds, but RBI's FCNR (B) window and ECLGS 5.0 act as counter-cyclical supports; house view — prefer Banks over NBFCs, PVBs over PSBs; top picks ICICI, RBL, KVB, Ujjivan SFB, SBI (banks) and Shriram, L&T Finance, AB Capital, CREDAG, Paytm (NBFC/fintech).

Credit growth: FY26 bank credit rebounded to 16.2% (NBFCs 19%); FY27E forecast 14-15% for banks (16.5% FY28E) and 18.5% for NBFCs, led by mid-corporate working capital, loans to NBFCs (+28% YoY), MSME and mortgages, while vehicle finance, gold and cards slow; deposits (12%) still lag loans (17.7% in Jun-26) with household deposit share (~59%) structurally declining.

FCNR (B) 2026 special window (8 Jun-30 Sep 2026): 3-5 year deposits swapped to INR with RBI bearing hedging cost, exempt from CRR/SLR; banks repriced FCNR rates from 2.5-4% to 6-7.1%; expected inflow USD50-100bn (1.8-3.7% of deposits vs USD29.6bn/2.6% in 2013), giving ~70bp spread benefit vs domestic deposits — biggest winners are deposit-strained large PVBs (HDFCB, ICICI, Axis) and banks with thin LCR buffers (HDFCB, IDFCB, Union).

ECLGS 5.0 relaunch targets INR2.55tn (1.2% of credit) of 100%-sovereign-guaranteed MSME working capital (90% for non-MSMEs; INR50bn airline carve-out), vs INR3.7tn disbursed 2020-23 with only ~4.8-5% lagged delinquency; SMA-2/NPA accounts excluded, and RBI granted risk-weight relief — key backstop for rising PSB MSME stress (PAR 31-90 up to 3.5% Apr-26 from 2.9% Mar-25).

Asset quality at decadal best (bank credit cost ~0.6% FY26, NBFC GNPA ~2.4%); retail PAR improving across products, but watch pockets: CV/CE stress from fuel/freight costs (MMFSL, Shriram, Chola, IIB, AU, Kotak most exposed), El Nino agri risk, IT job losses, and gold-loan NPA spikes at NBFCs (Manappuram Stage-3 4.9%) as gold corrects — mitigated by 57-63% LTVs; contingent buffers strongest at ICICI/Federal (1.2%/0.8% of loans).

ECL transition (effective 1 Apr 2027) is the key differentiator: limited for large PVBs given provision buffers (except IndusInd), but IIB, J&K, Yes, IDFCB, PNB and Canara may need capital; PSB earnings also face lower treasury gains (elevated ~7% G-sec) and NPA-recovery exhaustion, with PSB Consolidation 2.0 (Viksit Bharat committee; SBI, PNB, Canara, UCO as anchors) a rally-stopper — PSB RoA to moderate to ~0.9-1.0%.

Margins bottom out H1FY27: PVB NIMs to rise 3.7%→3.9% by FY28E on FCNR-led CoF relief and 89% EBLR-linked floating books (rate hikes, if any, are PVB-positive), while PSBs stay at ~2.7% on MCLR drag; ownership catalysts abound — ENBD acquired >60% of RBL (INR260bn infusion, Tier-1 >33%), SMBC 24.9% in Yes, MUFG ~20% in Shriram, Blackstone up to 9.99% in Federal, Bain in Manappuram — while management overhangs (HDFCB governance review, Kotak CEO Vaswani not seeking extension beyond Dec-26, BAF succession before Mar-28) cap large-cap valuations; ICICI's Bakhshi extended two years.

MFI recovery underway (industry GLP +12% FY27E/+17% FY28E after two down years; Ujjivan, CREDAG, LTF best asset quality) and fintech pivots to profitability — Paytm turned profitable in FY26 (merchant GMV +27% YoY, 15.1mn device subscriptions), initiated BUY TP INR1,400; structural decade themes: bank-NBFC regulatory convergence (upper-layer threshold moving to INR1tn assets), RBI's rejection of Ujjivan/Jana universal-bank licences, NOFHC ring-fencing, possible NBFC CEO-tenure caps, MDR-on-UPI debate, and AI as efficiency enhancer (tech spend now 10-11% of opex) rather than cost displacer.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Regulatory liquidity support (FCNR (B) swap window + ECLGS 5.0) materially de-risks bank funding and MSME asset quality for FY27, disproportionately benefiting deposit-constrained private banks.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: USD50-100bn potential FCNR inflow (1.8-3.7% of deposits) with CRR/SLR exemption yields ~70bp spread pickup and eases LCR strain at HDFCB, IDFCB, Union; ECLGS 5.0 (INR2.55tn, sovereign-guaranteed) caps MSME slippage risk. Track monthly FCNR (B) mobilisation vs the USD50bn base case and PSB MSME PAR 31-90 (3.5% Apr-26) for scheme efficacy.

key_takeaways.takeaway: System asset quality is at a decadal best but improvement is plateauing — West-Asia conflict and El Nino shift the credit-cost path from 'improving' to 'flat', not deteriorating.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Bank credit cost holds ~0.6% and NBFC ~1.8% in FY27E; risk concentrates in CV (fuel/freight squeeze on transporters), MSME and agri. A single weak monsoon historically doesn't spike agri NPAs — sustained El Nino cycles do. Track CV/tractor PAR and freight-rate-to-fuel spreads quarterly for MMFSL, Shriram, Chola, IIB, AU.

key_takeaways.takeaway: ECL transition (1 Apr 2027) is the single biggest balance-sheet differentiator: large PVBs absorb it via contingent buffers while IIB, Yes, IDFCB, PNB, Canara and J&K may need fresh capital.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: For weaker banks ECL hits both CET1 and the annual credit-cost run-rate just as PSB treasury gains fade (10y G-sec ~7%). Track disclosed ECL impact as % of net worth/RWA and post-ECL CET1; capital raises (incl. IIB strategic stake talk and Yes AT1 litigation of INR75bn) are the event risks.

key_takeaways.takeaway: PSB outperformance is peaking — 'best could be largely behind' as RoA moderates from ~1.2% to 0.9-1.0% and Consolidation 2.0 re-emerges as an event overhang.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Margin (MCLR repricing lag), lower treasury income and NPA-recovery exhaustion compress PSB earnings; merger integration (anchors: SBI, PNB, Canara, UCO) adds execution risk though cleaner balance sheets soften it. Track PSB NIM trajectory (2.7%) and any formal consolidation roadmap; SBI and Indian Bank (NNPA 0.15%, CET1 16.5%) are the quality outliers.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Foreign strategic capital is repricing mid-tier Indian banking credit profiles upward — ENBD/RBL, SMBC/Yes, MUFG/Shriram, Blackstone/Federal represent structural funding and governance upgrades.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: ENBD's >60% RBL stake (INR260bn, Tier-1 >33%) is transformational for RBL's funding cost and risk oversight; MUFG resolves Shriram promoter-transition risk. For credit work, these deals lower standalone default risk via parent support but watch integration and RBI ownership-dilution rules; track post-deal CoF and rating outlook changes at RBL and Shriram.

key_takeaways.takeaway: NBFC 'easy returns' are over: funding-cost tailwind has faded, bank-borrowing (37% of NBFC funding) is repricing up via MCLR hikes, and growth/margin convergence with banks compresses the sector's excess spread.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: NBFC NIMs flat at ~6.1% FY27E; AUM growth moderates to ~18.5%. Second-line turnarounds (Shriram RoA 3.8-4.2%, LTF retail now ~98% of AUM targeting 20% AUM CAGR under Lakshya 2031, AB Capital, CREDAG RoA 4.2-4.7%) have the RoA momentum; Muthoot's 13.1% Stage-3 PCR is a flagged risk. Track 3M CP rates and bank MCLR pass-through to NBFC CoF.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Bank deposit franchises face a structural erosion — savers turning investors keeps deposit growth (12%) below credit growth (17.7%), making liability strength the binding constraint on FY27-28 growth.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Household deposit share ~59% and individual-deposit share declining from 77.2% to 51.7% long-term; FCNR is a temporary bridge, not a cure. Banks that convert FCNR windfall into durable retail franchises win; track CASA growth and credit-deposit ratio normalisation post the Sep-26 window close.

key_takeaways.takeaway: MFI cycle has turned: after two years of GLP contraction from MFIN guardrails, growth resumes (+12% FY27E, +17% FY28E) with borrower deleveraging done — Ujjivan, Bandhan, CREDAG are the recovery plays.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: PAR buckets have normalised and ~8.3% of customers migrated from JLG to individual loans; banks ceded MFI share (45%→39%) to NBFC-MFIs. Ujjivan's 90+ DPD lowest among peers; RBI's universal-licence rejection (Ujjivan, Jana) forces faster diversification (secured already 49.4%). Track MFI PAR 31-60 re-acceleration and CREDAG promoter stake-sale overhang.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Fee income faces a regulatory reset — RBI mis-selling/disclosure salvoes will compress bancassurance and distribution fees, pushing banks toward core lending/transaction fees just as treasury gains fade.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Third-party distribution fee cuts hit PVBs with high banca dependence; PSBs simultaneously lose treasury support from elevated G-sec yields. Watch non-interest income/assets trend and PSL certificate fees (a rising offset for Canara, Union, Indian Bank).

key_takeaways.takeaway: Paytm's turnaround post bank-licence suspension validates the fintech monetisation pivot — profitability, not GMV, now drives valuation and funding access.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: FY26 PAT INR5.6bn, payment processing margin >4bp, EPS to grow 131% in FY27E; PA licence restored online-merchant onboarding. MDR reintroduction on UPI (86% of P2M volumes are sub-INR500) remains a policy call option, likely calibrated to >INR3,000 transactions if it comes. Track lending-distribution take rates and any MDR policy announcement.

entities_impacted.impact: Architect of the counter-cyclical package (FCNR (B) swap window with RBI-borne hedging cost, ECLGS 5.0 risk-weight relief) and of structural tightening: ECL from Apr-2027, NBFC upper-layer redefinition at INR1tn assets, universal-licence gatekeeping, potential NBFC CEO-tenure and promoter-ownership norms.

Top sector pick (BUY, TP INR1,800): peer-best RoA 2.1-2.2%, strongest contingent buffer (1.2% of loans), lowest ECL impact, and management risk resolved via Bakhshi's two-year extension — premium funding access intact.

BUY (TP INR1,025) but governance/board saga and MD-term uncertainty (beyond Oct-26) cap valuations; lower LCR buffer makes it a prime FCNR beneficiary; contingent buffer could release provisions post-ECL.
ENBD's >60% acquisition and INR260bn infusion lift Tier-1 above 33%, cut funding cost and add parent support — RoA path 0.5% (FY26) to 1.2-1.5% (FY28-29E); initiated BUY TP INR470.

Initiated REDUCE (TP INR22): core profitability reliant on ARC recoveries (SR pool 1.27% of loans), unresolved INR75bn AT1 litigation, ECL pressure on modest CET1 (13.8%) may force capital raise despite SMBC's 24.9% stake.

Post accounting/derivative discrepancies, new CEO Rajiv Anand rebuilding management; highest ECL vulnerability among large PVBs — may need strategic/ECL-absorption capital; RoA recovery 0.8%→1.4% by FY29E underpins BUY.
Preferred PSB (BUY, TP INR1,250): ~1% RoA, ~15% RoE sustained, peer-best GNPA; low probability of being dragged into consolidation, and scale absorbs ECL.
Initiated REDUCE (TP INR165): sub-optimal deposit growth (2% YoY), contracting margins, RoA fading 1.2%→0.9%; low LCR buffer and consolidation exposure add risk.
MUFG ~20% stake de-risks promoter transition and funds growth; diversified from used-CV to retail with RoA rising to 3.8-4.2%, though CV-cycle stress from fuel costs is the near-term watch item.

HOLD — growth normalised to ~22-24%, RoA steady at 4.0%, but Rajeev Jain succession must be resolved before Mar-28; possible regulatory glare on NBFC CEO tenure; AI adoption (20mn calls analysed) now a revenue tool.
Turned profitable FY26 after PPBL licence suspension; PA licence, 15.1mn device subscriptions and lending re-acceleration drive 131% FY27E EPS growth; initiated BUY TP INR1,400.
RoA moderation to ~0.9-1.0% on margin/treasury/recovery fade; ECL transition and Consolidation 2.0 are the twin overhangs, partly offset by ECLGS 5.0 support for their larger MSME books.

Growth 19%→18.5% with flat NIMs as bank-borrowing costs (37% of funding mix) reprice up; fresh stress pockets in CV/SME/business loans from West-Asia conflict and El Nino; regulatory convergence with banks accelerating.

learning: Quantify FCNR-type swap windows via the lendable-funds arithmetic: CRR/SLR exemption converts an equal-rate deposit into ~70bp of extra spread (100% lendable vs 79%), which is why such windows disproportionately help deposit-constrained, low-LCR private banks — track mobilisation as % of deposits (2013 benchmark: 2.6%) rather than headline USD flows.

Sovereign-guaranteed emergency credit (ECLGS) is not automatic evergreening risk: the 2020-23 vintage defaulted at only ~4.8-5% on a lag, and ECLGS 5.0's exclusion of SMA-2 accounts and working-capital-only design further reduce 'under-carpeting' — assess scheme design (eligibility cut-offs, guarantee %) before penalising banks for guaranteed-book growth.

In floating-rate systems, rate direction cuts opposite ways by benchmark mix: PVBs (89% EBLR/repo-linked) benefit from hikes and bleed first on cuts, while PSBs (~50% MCLR) lag both ways — margin forecasting must start from the benchmark composition table, not headline NIM.

For ECL-regime comparisons, contingent provisions as % of loans and post-transition CET1 (not GNPA) are the discriminating credit metrics; also prefer RoRWA over RoA to compare banks with different risk-weight densities — the report shows KVB/Indian Bank screen better on RoRWA than raw RoA suggests.
